item.title: Bobby Weaver pleads guilty to murder of man buried inside freezer in Ipswich backyard
item.description: Bobby Weaver pleaded guilty to murder more than seven years after the death of 58-year-old David Thornton, whose body was found buried in a freezer in his Ipswich backyard.

item.title: Australians expected to generate more solar panel waste each year by 2030, inquiry hears
item.description: Australians are expected to throw away 90,000 tonnes of solar panel waste each year by 2030, a federal government hearing has been told.

item.title: Algal bloom tracks further north up the Yorke Peninsula, sparking concern for stingrays
item.description: Evidence of South Australia's harmful algal bloom has been detected in previously untouched parts of the Spencer Gulf days after rays were seen gathering in shallow waters.

item.title: Cyclist's death deemed 'foreseeable' after warning about dangerous tree went unaddressed, coroner finds
item.description: A cyclist’s death on a popular Canberra path could have been prevented if the ACT government had acted promptly on a public warning about a dangerous overhanging branch, coronial findings reveal.

item.title: Australians reveal toll of fuel price rises on their lives as cost of living goes up
item.description: The ABC asked how you were coping with fuel prices and here's what we heard back.

item.title: What to know about the four Artemis II astronauts heading to the Moon
item.description: The Artemis II mission to the Moon could take four astronauts further from Earth than any human has gone before. Here's what we know about them.

item.title: Conservation group plans to replant Daintree Rainforest trees on former sugarcane land
item.description: A conservation group's plans to replant species from the World Heritage-listed Daintree Rainforest on land used for growing sugar have not won everybody over.

item.title: There's only been a handful of national addresses. Anthony Albanese has now made another
item.description: Pandemics, wars, and landmark moments have been immortalised in addresses to the nation. Anthony Albanese has become one of the few prime ministers to employ the form.

item.title: Police shoot man during welfare check in Brisbane's north
item.description: A 19-year-old man has been shot by police after officers were called to an Arana Hills address for a welfare check requested by his mother.

item.title: Murder charge against partner of Mullumbimby's Ashleigh Grice is dropped
item.description: A murder charge has been withdrawn against a Mullumbimby man over the death of his partner, Ashleigh Grice, after a post-mortem examination found the 23-year-old woman died of "mixed drug effects".

item.title: Court makes landmark decision after illegal logging in NSW state forest
item.description: Traditional owners say a $450,000 fine will not replace the six centuries-old giant trees illegally felled by NSW Forestry Corporation.

item.title: Brisbane Broncos senior leaders decider to stand down winger Josiah Karapani
item.description: Brisbane captain Adam Reynolds says the club's senior leaders took a non-negotiable stance on culture and behaviour to stand down winger Josiah Karapani for one match.

item.title: Track the latest petrol and diesel prices around Australia
item.description: See what's happening with petrol and diesel prices near you, updated constantly.

item.title: Graham Arnold coaches Iraq to first FIFA World Cup appearance in 40 years
item.description: Former Socceroos coach Graham Arnold has guided Iraq to their first FIFA World Cup appearance in 40 years with a playoff win over Boliva.

item.title: Tuesday no longer cheapest day for petrol in Perth as Middle East war disrupts fuel price cycle
item.description: Perth motorists can no longer rely on "cheap Tuesday" fuel prices and are urged to shop around every day as the Middle East war disrupts global supply chains.

item.title: WA government to use emergency powers to force fuel companies to reveal supply chain information
item.description: The Western Australian government will use emergency powers to force fuel companies to provide information about their supply chains in an effort to keep fuel flowing through the state.

item.title: Murder trial begins over 2024 death of Alice Springs teenager Yiel Gatluak
item.description: Jurors in the trial for the alleged 2024 murder of Yiel Gatluak in Alice Springs have heard they will see "a degree of violence most of you probably won't be accustomed to".

item.title: Anthropic boss makes big call on Australian copyright as artists say pay up
item.description: The AI company's billionaire boss says he is not trying to convince Australia to change its mind on protecting the copyright of artists.

item.title: Victim speaks out after sexual assaults on remote farm in north-east Victoria
item.description: A sexual assault survivor says the sentence handed to a well-known farmer and former community leader who groped and kissed her on his remote Victorian property is disappointing.

item.title: Jury shown crucial exhibits on final day of evidence in Irma Palasics cold case murder trial
item.description: A milk container and water jug carrying DNA evidence were revealed to jurors as a decades-old murder trial reached its final stage.

item.title: Class action alleges GrainCorp had 'expand first, fix problems later' approach to production
item.description: A class action of 18 people from the small Victorian town of Numurkah alleges that agribusiness giant GrainCorp's increased production at the site has led to offensive noise and odour for residents.

item.title: Live Nation's Love Song 2026 finds gen Z embrace retro tech, scrapbooking, in-person connection
item.description: Gen Z is embracing retro technology like iPods, vinyl and Polaroid cameras and craves in-person connection more than any other generation, according to a Live Nation study.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Albanese urges Australians to 'live life as normal' during fuel crisis in rare national address
item.description: Anthony Albanese asks Australians to trade their cars for public transport if they can, while reassuring those who need to drive they can continue to do so amid the global oil crisis.

item.title: Anthony Albanese delivers address to the nation on fuel crisis
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australian rugby union coach Mick Byrne ends tenure as Fiji boss just 18 months out from World Cup
item.description: Mick Byrne steps down from his role as head coach of the Fiji national rugby team after two years in charge.

item.title: Anthony Albanese's national address contained reassurance on the fuel crisis — and a warning shot
item.description: The prime minister risks criticism for using the rare measure of a national address to urge calm without announcing any new measures.

item.title: Michael Voss says Carlton hurting but committed to improving against North Melbourne
item.description: Carlton head coach Michael Voss has said there is not a "silver bullet" to fix the club's second-half blues, following three successive fade-outs to start the season.

item.title: Five teenagers arrested after multiple home invasions across Brisbane and Gold Coast
item.description: Multiple people were injured during the series of break-and-enter offences across South Brisbane and the Gold Coast.

item.title: Prime Minister urges Australians to save fuel
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Global uncertainty a reason to 'press ahead' with urgent reform, Albanese says
item.description: The prime minister uses a speech at the National Press Club to make his pitch for "urgent" change amid significant international uncertainty caused by the Middle East war.

item.title: WA man charged with terrorism offences reflected on Bondi attacks in diary, court told
item.description: Jayson Joseph Michaels, 20, had reflected on the Bondi attacks in his diary and was allegedly planning a "day of justice", a Perth court hears.

item.title: At least four killed in daytime Russian drone strikes across Ukraine
item.description: Both sides are launching drone attacks as the war between Russia and Ukraine drags into its fifth year.

item.title: Anthony Albanese wants Australians to believe he has a fuel plan
item.description: With no realistic end in sight and a whole lot of economic pain to come, the PM's decision to make a direct plea to the nation was not one taken lightly.

item.title: Parents demand action after falling concrete closes entire floor of Collingwood College
item.description: Collingwood College is sending students to another campus for science classes after concrete fell from its water-damaged roof and forced the closure of an entire floor of its main building.

item.title: Know the difference between a pomologist and an oologist? We have the quiz for you
item.description: Australia’s museums hold a treasure trove of odd facts, native animals and even burlesque history. So how much do you know about the things hidden in our nation’s vaults?

item.title: From Children's Laureate to Treehouse on TV, how Andy Griffiths' quirky kids books captured the country
item.description: Andy Griffiths has written 43 books, is the Children's Laureate, and is about to turn his Treehouse series into an ABC TV show. So what inspires the former punk and teacher to keep creating quirky tales for kids?

item.title: The new moonshot
item.description: If all goes well for NASA's mission, the astronauts aboard could fly the furthest that humans have ever gone. What should we be looking out for?

item.title: One Nation picked candidates accused of domestic abuse, antisemitism
item.description: A One Nation candidate in the South Australian election was accused of domestic abuse, bullying and harassment, while another made racist, antisemitic and homophobic social media posts, raising fresh questions about the party's vetting processes as it surges in popularity.

item.title: What Australia can learn from France's approach to free public transport
item.description: There are growing calls in Australia for free public transport amid the Iran war with two states putting in place free services on a temporary basis. But in France, almost 50 cities have free public transport.

item.title: US burning through years' worth of critical weapons stockpiles in Iran
item.description: Analysts estimate the US is burning through high-end weapons in Iran at a rate that could take years to replenish, risking the West's military readiness for other potential conflicts.

item.title: Singapore's major oil source is blocked and experts warn Australians will pay
item.description: Singapore is exposed to the oil shock from Iran's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, and its response will shape the fuel crisis in Australia.

item.title: Australia wasting migrant talent on an 'industrial scale', former Treasury secretary says
item.description: Australia is failing to recognise the qualifications of highly trained permanent migrants, former Treasury secretary Martin Parkinson says.

item.title: Forecast of calm Easter weather for most of Australia, but another cyclone looms
item.description: Australia has just been soaked by its third wettest February-March on record, but for most of the country, skies have cleared up just in time for Easter.

item.title: Sydney Royal Easter Show to go on, despite rising fuel costs
item.description: The country has come to the city for the Royal Easter Show and despite the cost of the trip blowing out amid high fuel prices, the exhibitors say they would not miss it.

item.title: Ikara-Flinders Ranges Easter tourism 'worse than COVID', while other regional towns see spike
item.description: An Ikara-Flinders Ranges tourism operator says he is experiencing more cancellations than bookings this Easter as fuel shortage anxiety keep travellers away, while other regional towns expect to be booked out.

item.title: Reopening Hormuz by force 'unrealistic', says Macron — as it happened
item.description: The French president has expressed scepticism about how the key waterway could be reopened as war in the Middle East continues.

item.title: Dezi Freeman's time on the run puts sovereign citizens in the spotlight
item.description: As detectives investigate whether the sovereign citizen community helped harbour police killer Dezi Freeman, researchers say the anti-government movement appears to be surging in regional Australia.

item.title: Sameer Rizvi's Capitals post six-wicket IPL win over Mitch Marsh's Super Giants
item.description: Mitch Marsh launches his Indian Premier League season with three big sixes, but it was not enough to help the Lucknow Super Giants in a six-wicket loss to Delhi Capitals.

item.title: Outback GPs warn delays in patient data across services put lives at risk
item.description: Rural GPs say privacy barriers and fragmented systems mean vital patient information can take over a month to access, putting patients at risk.

item.title: Federal government threatens to use powers to limit gas exports unless producers preserve enough for local use
item.description: It is the first time the government has taken a step towards using the powers it has to limit exports, and it comes at a time when the profits from those exports appear set to surge.

item.title: As it happened: ASX drops on back of Trump speech, oil back up
item.description: ASX drops back following President Donald Trump's speech on Iran. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News markets blog.

item.title: Woman killed by bus in Adelaide CBD in third pedestrian crash in three days
item.description: Witnesses are being encouraged to seek counselling after a 39-year-old died when she was hit by an Adelaide Metro bus on King William Road in the Adelaide CBD. It comes after two other pedestrians were hit and injured in similar incidents earlier in the week.

item.title: England's Harry Brook and Jacob Bethell let off with warning over New Zealand nightclub altercation
item.description: Harry Brook and Jacob Bethell escape with a caution notice following an investigation into a nightclub incident in ‌New Zealand late ⁠last year.

item.title: Look back on the launch of NASA's first Moon mission in half a century
item.description: Four astronauts are on board the Orion capsule, orbiting Earth as humanity seeks to return to the Moon for the first time in 54 years. Take a look back at how the day's events unfolded.

item.title: Bumper season brings cheaper avocados, 'record' exports, but growers pay price
item.description: A bumper avocado season is creating a perfect storm for cheaper produce and record export volumes, but growers are doing it tough.

item.title: Daly River and Palumpa flood evacuees transferred to Batchelor, doubling town's population overnight
item.description: Flood evacuees from Daly River and Palumpa have been transferred from Darwin to the small town of Batchelor, where they could stay for months. Locals say they were not consulted before the town's population doubled overnight.

item.title: Keir Starmer and European leaders defy Donald Trump as cost of war hits home
item.description: Early on, world leaders went out of their way to placate Donald Trump. But as the war with Iran drags into a second month, the UK and European leaders are finding their voices.

item.title: Astronauts enter space capsule ahead of Artemis II blast-off
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Artemis II crew appear to be in high spirits ahead of moon mission
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Forestry company parks electric truck despite spike in diesel prices
item.description: A 2.5-year trial of an electric truck in SA's forestry industry has concluded with the truck now parked, finding it was able to do the job but wasn't cost effective, even with high fuel prices.

item.title: Thousands lost to cancellations, expenses surge as fuel crisis bites southern Queensland tourism sector
item.description: Tourism businesses say they’re bearing the brunt of surging fuel prices, with some losing thousands to booking cancellations and extra operational costs, while others field back-to-back calls from anxious tourists about fuel availability.

item.title: Arsenal defeats Chelsea 3-2 on aggregate in Women's Champions League as Matildas face off
item.description: Steph Catley and Caitlin Foord come up against Matildas teammates Sam Kerr and Ellie Carpenter, as Arsenal prevails in its UEFA Women's Champions League quarterfinal against Chelsea.

item.title: How a youth outreach program is keeping teens engaged in Kalumburu
item.description: Kalumburu youth workers are giving teens the chance to get away from screens, be on country and connect with nature.

item.title: When work becomes your identity, is it time to nurture your non-work self?
item.description: The centrality of work in our lives can't be denied, especially when Australians are working longer hours than ever before. But short of quitting our jobs, how do we shift the balance?

item.title: Artemis II blasts off from launch pad
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: DNA links US serial killer Ted Bundy to unsolved 1974 murder
item.description: New DNA testing definitively links infamous serial killer Ted Bundy to the unsolved death of a US teenager in 1974.

item.title: Storm NRL forward Tui Kamikamica leaves hospital after suffering suspected stroke
item.description: The club says Tui Kamikamica will now "commence a period of inpatient rehabilitation" after the "medical episode" on Monday.

item.title: No Easter opening for WA's landmark Gloucester Tree climb with rebuild delayed
item.description: Plans to reopen the climb up the Gloucester Tree by the Easter long weekend have fallen by the wayside as work continues to make it safer for visitors.

item.title: Bid to suppress identities of Bondi gunman Naveed Akram's mother, siblings fails in NSW court
item.description: Lawyers for Bondi gunman Naveed Akram fail in a bid to have the identities of his mother, brother and sister protected by a decades-long court suppression order on safety grounds.

item.title: Super Mario Galaxy Movie review: The only way out is through
item.description: Even with downgraded expectations, the Nintendo sequel is little more than Easter eggs and bright colours. But that won't stop it from filling cinemas.

item.title: Relocating tiger sharks may reduce short-term risk of shark bites, research suggests
item.description: A new study analysing the behaviour of tiger sharks relocated from the Great Barrier Reef Marine Park under the Queensland Shark Control Program shows tiger sharks relocated from swimming beaches generally return to a beach within about three months.

item.title: Crypto exchange platform markets in Australia despite ASIC intervention
item.description: A major cryptocurrency platform is operating like "gambling" in Australia, according to experts, despite new regulatory laws.

item.title: Powerful magnitude-7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia, tsunami warning issued
item.description: The quake, which hit early today, had a depth of 35 kilometres and its epicentre was 127km west-north-west of Ternate, Indonesia, the US Geological Survey said.

item.title: More SA general practices charging extra administration fees to cover payroll tax
item.description: Patients are being asked to make an additional, separate transaction at general practices across Adelaide of between $5 and $10 to cover increased operating costs following the implementation of payroll tax for contracted doctors in 2024.

item.title: Australia to join 34 countries — but not the US — in meeting on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Australia will join a virtual meeting of 35 countries to discuss plans to reopen and secure the Strait of Hormuz, and ease the oil shock rocking global markets.

item.title: States and territories agree to further fuel excise cut, using extra GST revenue
item.description: Premiers and chief ministers agree to use an estimated $400 million in extra GST revenue to fund a 5.7 per litre cut to the fuel excise, on top of the Commonwealth's 26.3 cent per litre relief.

item.title: Still no trial date for Shyanne-Lee Tatnell accused killer Christopher Mark Jordan
item.description: The man accused of murdering Tasmanian teenager Shyanne-Lee Tatnell remains without a trial date, almost three years since he was charged.

item.title: Carlton's poor second halves are the talk of the AFL, so what is going wrong for the Blues?
item.description: Carlton's horrible second halves have become a costly trend which has the team struggling and Michael Voss fighting for his job. This is what is going wrong after half-time for the Blues.

item.title: In pictures: Artemis II NASA's first mission to the Moon in decades
item.description: For the first time in more than 50 years NASA has sent astronauts around the Moon, marking a major milestone in a new era of space exploration.

item.title: Albanese unveils long-awaited response to landmark gambling reform report
item.description: Gambling ads to be partially restricted on television and radio, with opt-out rules for online promotions and a ban in stadiums and on jerseys under reforms unveiled by Labor.

item.title: 'Full of contradictions': John Lyons on Donald Trump's national address
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Essendon Bombers coach Brad Scott adamant club is not rebuilding despite losing streak
item.description: Essendon coach Brad Scott has a simple message for anyone who thinks his struggling AFL club is in a "rebuild" phase.

item.title: Backpackers jobless after ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle shuts down Exmouth ahead of Easter holiday
item.description: In a WA town long reliant on backpackers, there may be few workers left by the time it is ready to reopen for business.

item.title: Federal court class action filed against Kmart alleges 'systemic' wage underpayment
item.description: Kmart managers have alleged they were routinely required to work "well beyond" their rostered hours without receiving overtime rates, penalty rates, or prescribed allowances.

item.title: Donald Trump threatens to hit Iran 'extremely hard' over next few weeks
item.description: In his first national address since the start of the war, President Donald Trump says the US will hit Iran hard over the next two to three weeks, and tells allies they need to secure the Strait of Hormuz if they want to calm oil markets.

item.title: Neo-Nazi Joel Davis granted bail over alleged threats to 'rhetorically rape' Allegra Spender
item.description: Neo-Nazi Joel Davis has been released from custody on bail after a Sydney court heard he only gets to shower once every four days while on remand.

item.title: When is my child old enough to come running with me?
item.description: If you enjoy running and your child has shown interest in coming along, you might be wondering when they're old enough to start. Experts say there are a few ways you can determine if they're ready.

item.title: Your Good Friday fish and chips may be coming from as far away as South Africa this Easter
item.description: This Easter is the first in Western Australia under tough new demersal fishing rules. Seafood retailers say the change in supply and price is noticeable.

item.title: COVID vaccine injury victims speak out about slow claims process and threats over complaints made to Services Australia
item.description: For years these vaccine-injured Australians have been trying to get compensation from a 'torturous' government compensation scheme.

item.title: The PM gave the all-clear to 'enjoy your Easter'. Here's what you should know first about fuel
item.description: Thanks to fuel supply concerns your Easter trip may not look like normal. Here's what you need to know before hitting the road.

item.title: Sam Kerr in, Mary Fowler not part of Matildas squad for FIFA Series in Kenya
item.description: Sam Kerr has been named, but the Matildas will be missing a number of their other big guns when they travel to Kenya for the team's maiden FIFA Series in April.

item.title: Former Newington College teacher Benjamin David Collinge granted bail on child grooming charges
item.description: A former casual teacher at the prestigious Sydney school has been granted bail after his arrest over allegedly grooming a teenage girl last month.

item.title: 'Krill wars' back on in Southern Ocean as activist ship Bandero deliberately collides with Aker QRILL trawler
item.description: An environmental activist group’s ship has deliberately collided with a large fishing trawler in Antarctic waters. The activists say it was a "gentle nudge" but the trawler's owner says a line has been crossed.

item.title: NT police confirm external police review into Kumanjayi White death in custody complete
item.description: NT police have received the independent use of force review into the death in custody of Kumanjayi White in Alice Springs in 2025.

item.title: Trump's speech was significant for what he did not say
item.description: The US president's national address was largely a cobble-together of past talking points and Truth Social posts. Perhaps the most notable takeaways relate to what went unmentioned.

item.title: Albanese tries to get fuel message out as Chalmers's budget countdown begins
item.description: This week in Canberra, the prime minister had a message for Australians, his treasurer laid the groundwork for the upcoming federal budget and Angus Taylor's car was stolen.

item.title: Former NSW Police officer to appeal conviction for child sex abuse
item.description: A former NSW Police officer convicted of sexually assaulting his teenage step daughter has had his sentence suspended pending a Supreme Court appeal.

item.title: One Nation wins its fourth seat in SA's lower house following Narungga recount
item.description: The seat of Narungga was so close it required a recount, and has now been won by One Nation candidate Chantelle Thomas by a margin of 58 votes over the Liberals.

item.title: Tim Tszyu ready to revive world title dream with Easter Sunday bout against Denis Nurja
item.description: Tim Tszyu is promising no more slip-ups while candidly confessing to "falling apart" after relinquishing his world title in the infamous Sebastian Fundora bloodbath in Las Vegas.

item.title: Angus Taylor delivers address to the nation on fuel crisis
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Angus Taylor uses national address to call on Australians to 'look after ourselves'
item.description: Angus Taylor has issued a return serve to the prime minister's national address by urging people to "put Australia first" while accusing the government of providing a lack of clarity about the ongoing fuel crisis.

item.title: New recruits impress for Brisbane as Collingwood star Nick Daicos misses match due to corked calf
item.description: Collingwood is unsure whether Nick Daicos will spend time on the sidelines after the star midfielder was forced out of Thursday night's match with a calf injury.

item.title: Mickayla Kerim avoids jail time for being accessory to bestiality offences
item.description: The NSW woman has been convicted of being an accessory to bestiality offences involving her then-husband.

item.title: Police officers swim out to rescue woman in Perth's Swan River
item.description: Two police officers who swam out into Perth's Swan River early on Thursday to help rescue a woman will be recommended for a bravery award.

item.title: Thursday finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Foran relieved after Manly's 'whirlwind week' ends with emphatic victory
item.description: Less than seven days after taking over from sacked coach Anthony Seibold, Kieran Foran takes immense pride in Manly's first win of the season.

item.title: Police decision to call off ambulance for Jeffrey Winmar scrutinised during custodial death inquest
item.description: The events leading up to the death of Indigenous father Jeffrey Winmar in police custody in Melbourne's east in 2023 are being examined through a coronial inquest.

item.title: Numurkah residents fearful after 'explosion' at GrainCorp factory, court hears
item.description: Residents in the Northern Victorian town of Numurkah have told a court of their fear after an early-morning "explosion" they claim occurred at the GrainCorp factory.

item.title: Matildas' players join Japan criticising 'inequality' in prize money from Asian Football Confederation
item.description: Fierce rivals on the field, the Matildas and Japan have joined forces to slam the Asian Football Confederation's prize money for the Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: Donald Trump promises the end of the Iran war is near
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: COVID vaccine claimants remain in administrative purgatory
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Discovering the depths of the Coral Sea
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi suffers suspected heart attack in prison, family says
item.description: Iranian activist and Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi has been repeatedly charged and imprisoned by the regime over the past 25 years.

item.title: Military planners to meet on Strait of Hormuz as UN warns of wider Middle East war
item.description: Military planners are set to meet after ministers from 40 countries, including Australia's Penny Wong, attended virtual talks on reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: A New York Times critic used AI to write his review — but criticism is deeply human
item.description: An author and freelance journalist has admitted to using AI to help him write a book review for the New York Times.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A lost penguin, successful NASA rocket launch and address to the nation
item.description: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

item.title: Cate Blanchett reunites with Jim Jarmusch on anthology movie Father Mother Sister Brother
item.description: The complexities of family light up Jim Jarmusch's Golden Lion-winner, Father Mother Sister Brother, which features Blanchett alongside Adam Driver and Tom Waits.

item.title: Trump is promising to hit Iran 'extremely hard' and his assets in the Middle East give him options
item.description: While Donald Trump talks about ending and winning the war in Iran, major US military assets are still on their way to the Middle East. These are the options they give him.

item.title: Price ceiling for rising oil prices comes with a caveat
item.description: Trying to guess exactly where the oil price tops out is fast becoming the chocolate wheel of choice among politicians, economists and oil experts.

item.title: What the shifting language of Australia's leaders reveals about the Iran war
item.description: With the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, world oil markets rattled and no obvious off-ramp to the hostilities in sight, the major parties have dialled back their initial optimism.

item.title: Future fuel plan should be central to Albanese government's reform agenda
item.description: You could view the present contest between the Albanese government and the Taylor-Canavan opposition as pragmatism versus populism.

item.title: How the fuel crisis is hurting small regional Australian tourist towns like Foster
item.description: In one community, the effects of the fuel crisis are not always where you would think.

item.title: Increasing ACL injuries partly attributed to early specialisation in sport
item.description: Australia has one of the highest rates of ACL injuries per number of people in the world, and younger people have the highest chance of tearing the ligament.

item.title: Regional drivers shift gears to electric vehicles as fuel prices climb
item.description: EV uptake in regional Victoria is on the rise, but is everyone willing to make a switch?

item.title: Australia sources most of its jet fuel from Asia, but suppliers are feeling the pinch
item.description: Australia imports most of its jet fuel from Asia, particularly China, Singapore and South Korea. But those countries rely on the Middle East for crude oil, meaning they are particularly vulnerable to the impacts of war.

item.title: 'Rubber time', bottoms up and reading the silence: What's doing business like in Asia?
item.description: In Australia, a good deal builds relationships. In Asia, the opposite is often true. Here are some other cultural differences to understand.

item.title: South Australian MP Jason Virgo's switch from the Australian Sex Party to One Nation
item.description: Sixteen years before Jason Virgo won the SA seat of MacKillop for One Nation in the state's south-east, he twice ran for parliament as an Australian Sex Party candidate.

item.title: US President Donald Trump puts 100pc tariff on some pharmaceuticals
item.description: The US president's latest order shows he remains committed to his aggressive tariff regime despite February's Supreme Court ruling that invalidated his centrepiece "Liberation Day" tariffs.

item.title: What to know about fuel levies and surcharges before paying one
item.description: Shoppers may have noticed an extra charge at the checkout as businesses seek to offset rising operational costs associated with the fuel price hike. Here is what you need to know.

item.title: Fuel crisis highlights gaps in Western Sydney's public transport network
item.description: Mount Druitt residents would happily use public transport to avoid high fuel prices, but say it is not an option due to unreliable services.

item.title: Tiger Woods heard telling police he was 'just talking to the president' after Florida car crash
item.description: Police have released body camera footage showing Tiger Woods's arrest after he crashed his SUV last week in Florida.

item.title: Cyclone could form on Saturday, but that's not clouding Qld long weekend
item.description: Expect average temperatures and good weather inland, with scattered showers along the coast as Queensland heads into the Easter long weekend.

item.title: Saints' Lance Collard to face tribunal for alleged homophobic slur in VFL match
item.description: St Kilda forward Lance Collard will front a disciplinary tribunal hearing next week after he allegedly used a homophobic slur during a VFL match.

item.title: Invasive feral hog deer discovered in New South Wales but authorities confident of eradication
item.description: Standing only 70 centimetres tall, the hog deer has not been seen in the state for more than a decade, but poses a significant environmental threat.

item.title: Bret McKenzie on Flight of the Conchords reunion, Oscar win and songs without jokes
item.description: From Flight of the Conchords to Oscar-winning film and TV songwriting, Bret McKenzie always finds the fun in his career.

item.title: What lowering the diesel flash point means for Australia's refineries
item.description: The Australian government has lowered the diesel flashpoint for the next six months by one degree Celsius — but what does that actually mean?

item.title: Fuel crisis driving some Tasmanian farmers to consider abandoning harvests
item.description: Tasmanian farmers are considering not harvesting crops like broccoli and cauliflower because increasing freight costs are proving to be so debilitating.

item.title: Matildas captain Sam Kerr shoots down Chelsea exit rumour after report of potential US move
item.description: Matildas captain Sam Kerr is quick to cast doubt on a report she will leave Chelsea at the end of the season to join US club Denver Summit.

item.title: SA fishers demand urgent action to protect stocks, fearing long-term wipeout
item.description: Easter is a popular time for consumers to purchase fish, but commercial fishers in South Australia say stocks have been decimated by the algal bloom. They fear it will get worse without further action.

item.title: WA holiday-makers stick closer to home over fuel supply fears
item.description: Accommodation providers in regional WA tourism hotspots see a high number of cancellations, as holiday-makers choose to stay close to home due to fuel prices.

item.title: Cameron Green's IPL woes for Knight Riders continue as Travis Head fires for Sunrisers
item.description: Cameron Green is unable to arrest his shaky start to the Indian Premier League season, but Travis Head blasts back to his best.

item.title: 'Rare parasitic fungus' behind outbreak of severe conjunctivitis in Top End wet season footballers
item.description: Health authorities say a rare water-borne pathogen is behind the recent outbreak of severe conjunctivitis in Top End football clubs, with a number of Darwin playing fields deemed high-risk infection sites.

item.title: Police arrest a dozen people in operation targeting soaring Ballarat car thefts
item.description: Police have arrested 12 people during a major operation launched in response to a rise in car thefts in Ballarat.

item.title: What to watch, read and listen to this weekend: Aaron Chen, Super Mario Galaxy and Robyn
item.description: Break out the chocolate, pick up the remote: we've got you covered for Easter weekend.

item.title: Fuel crisis, cyclones, a teen hero — here are the photos that tell WA's stories in February and March
item.description: Here are some of our top photo picks from the past two months of news coverage.

item.title: Judge dismisses Blake Lively's sexual harassment claims against It Ends With Us co-star Justin Baldoni
item.description: A US federal court judge tosses out Blake Lively's sexual harassment claims against It Ends With Us co-star Justin Baldoni, but leaves three more claims intact.

item.title: Port Macquarie family travels overseas for unproven stem-cell treatment as experts urge caution
item.description: A young mother who travelled overseas for controversial stem cell therapy hopes new treatments will one day be available in Australia, but experts warn there's still a "long way" to go.

item.title: US Army's top uniformed officer asked to step down as countries discuss coalition to reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The ousting comes nearly five weeks into US-Israeli attacks on Iran and with no clear timeline from President Donald Trump on when the war may end.

item.title: Australia scores nine-wicket win over West Indies in final ODI in St Kitts
item.description: Alana King stars with the ball, as the Australian women's team finish their white-ball tour of West Indies with a resounding nine-wicket win in St Kitts.

item.title: US negotiator in 2015 Iran nuclear deal says Donald Trump 'delusional' on nuclear and regime change
item.description: Robert Malley, who negotiated the 2015 nuclear deal with Iran, says Donald Trump's address to the US this week was designed to deceive.

item.title: Motorbike rider killed in traffic light crash at Ovingham, driver dies days after Nuriootpa collision
item.description: A motorcyclist has died at Ovingham in Adelaide's inner north, hours after another rider was flown to hospital with life-threatening injuries sustained during a separate crash.

item.title: Fitzroy River plan sparks concern among pastoralists and traditional owners
item.description: A long-awaited draft plan aimed at protecting one of Western Australia's largest rivers has been released for public consultation, but traditional owners, environmental groups and pastoralists say key changes are needed.

item.title: SA Electoral Commission apologises to staff over pay delay following state election
item.description: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has apologised to staff who have not been fully paid almost a fortnight after the state election, saying a payroll processing delay had caused the problem.

item.title: NASA Artemis II astronauts rocket towards moon after spending day orbiting Earth
item.description: The Orion capsule carrying four astronauts in NASA's Artemis II mission successfully fired its engines this morning, kicking the crew out of Earth's orbit and bound for the moon.

item.title: FIFA raises top ticket price for World Cup final to nearly $16,000 during glitch-hampered sales reopening
item.description: The top ticket price was $15,900, a sharp increase from the original $12,560 when FIFA sold tickets after the tournament draw in December.

item.title: Australians mark Good Friday with seafood, church services and a boat race
item.description: Australians begin the long weekend with church services, time outdoors and seafood-filled lunches.

item.title: Bank of America's $105 million settlement with Epstein victims wins preliminary approval
item.description: A US judge has granted preliminary approval to Bank of America's $105.3 million settlement with women who have accused the bank of facilitating their sexual abuse by Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Dockers claim thriller after Crow hits post inside final minute
item.description: Fremantle coughs up a five-goal lead before holding on in the final quarter after Adelaide's James Borlase hits the post with 30 seconds left on the clock.

item.title: Dirt bike rider, 14, among three dead in separate WA Easter long weekend crashes
item.description: The long weekend is off to a tragic start after two motorcyclists die in separate crashes near Perth, while a woman, 18, is killed in a four-wheel drive rollover in WA's north.

item.title: Westpac CEO warns 'there's a chance of a recession' as interest rates rise
item.description: Westpac's chief executive, Anthony Miller, says Australians need to acknowledge there is a growing risk the country could slip into recession, amid the Iran war-driven fuel crisis and rising interest rates.

item.title: Bellamy threatens to wield axe after Storm's humiliating loss to Panthers
item.description: Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy puts his players on notice after they were thrashed 50-10 by Penrith on Good Friday.

item.title: Keeping Trump and his MAGA base happy proved too tough for Pam Bondi
item.description: Pam Bondi and Kristi Noem were both fired after controversial tenures carrying out Donald Trump's instructions in two of the trickiest roles in his administration.

item.title: Iranian missile bypasses air defences in Petah Tikva, central Israel
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: National Institute of Dramatic Art's former director John Clark dies aged 93
item.description: A giant of Australian theatre who helped launch the careers of some of the country's biggest names of the stage has died aged 93.

item.title: Government says billions of litres of fuel are en route to Australia, but industry stakeholders aren't so optimistic
item.description: Those in trucking industry are not getting hopes up that fuel will arrive in Australia, after Energy Minister announces 53 ships are on their way.

item.title: Motorcyclist dies following crash on Monaro Highway in southern NSW
item.description: A highway has been closed in southern NSW after the state's first reported road death of the Easter long weekend, with the detour expected to add five hours' travel time for some motorists.

item.title: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing elected president by pro-military parliament
item.description: In a live broadcast of the vote count the former commander-in-chief comfortably passed the threshold required to win the presidency.

item.title: Queensland man jailed for 15 years after live-streaming child abuse
item.description: A North Lakes man is sentenced to jail after pleading guilty to seven child abuse offences. His arrest came after Australian authorities received a tip-off from the FBI.

item.title: Live: One crew member missing after two US jets shot down by Iran
item.description: Two US planes have been shot down by Iran with 2 crew members rescued while another remains missing. Follow live.

item.title: World loses patience with Trump as war is no longer just about Iran
item.description: Global leaders are giving up the pretence of diplomatic niceties as they lose patience with the US president's actions.

item.title: Best podcasts to listen to this Easter long weekend, from Expanse: The Nannup Four to Empire City
item.description: The podcasts we're listening to this weekend include an investigation into the Salt Path scandal and a personal look at the role of Australia in the end of apartheid.

item.title: How Gordi transformed a song meant for Alex Lahey into her stunning new single
item.description: Gordi's new single was originally meant to be for her partner. Thankfully, both artists know the importance of trusting their gut.

item.title: How former negotiators say Victoria Police would have prepared to descend on Dezi Freeman's hiding place
item.description: A former police hostage negotiator says he was sure Dezi Freeman's seven-month run from the law "was going to end in tears" and that the fugitive would never surrender.

item.title: Soaring gas prices from Iran war puts South-East Asian countries like the Philippines on the brink
item.description: Countries heavily reliant on imported oil are particularly vulnerable to the unfolding energy crisis caused by the US-Israel war with Iran. It's no more apparent than in South-East Asian countries like the Philippines.

item.title: Sailors trapped by Iran's Strait of Hormuz blockade plead for help
item.description: Interviews and first-hand accounts from sailors stranded in the Persian Gulf — under threat of drone attacks, missiles, and exploding boats — reveal an increasingly dire humanitarian crisis unfolding.

item.title: When does daylight saving time end in Australia? Here's your pocket guide
item.description: The air is getting cooler and the days are about to get shorter as daylight savings comes to an end this weekend.

item.title: Banubanu Beach Retreat damaged by Cyclone Narelle
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Some of the devastation caused by Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle around Carnarvon
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: The damage at Bullara Station near Exmouth after Cyclone Narelle.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle wreaked havoc across QLD, WA and the NT
item.description: From the very first news reports about Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle, it was clear this wasn't just another storm. This is the story of how the cyclone impacted the entire span of northern Australia and how communities came together to pick up the pieces.

item.title: Strategically critical Guam divided over growing US military presence
item.description: The Pacific Island of Guam sits at the centre of a struggle between the world's most powerful military and the right of its people to determine their own future.

item.title: Thinking of changing your private health insurance? Ask these five questions
item.description: The loopholes to look for if you're thinking about downgrading or changing your private health insurance.

item.title: One Nation leaves SA Liberals clinging to opposition status
item.description: The Liberal Party will have just one more seat than One Nation in South Australia’s lower house, leaving opposition leader Ashton Hurn little margin for error.

item.title: Ms Curran releasing one of the bats she rehabilitated.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australian grey-headed flying fox economic and ecological value of poo revealed
item.description: Bats are often thought of as smelly, ugly and noisy, but a study has revealed the economic and ecological value of one Australian species's poo.

item.title: Blues AFL coach Michael Voss unfazed about speculation over his future after Kangaroos loss
item.description: Michael Voss knows he faces more scrutiny over his role as Blues coach after his side endures another second-half meltdown.

item.title: Long-term travellers camp out or alter plans due to surging fuel prices
item.description: Surging diesel costs have caught many travellers off guard, forcing them to camp out, cancel, or push on and wear the hit to their hip pockets.

item.title: Court dog success has handlers calling for more volunteers
item.description: Therapy dogs in courthouses are helping victims and witnesses of crimes to confront some of their darkest moments, but volunteers say many more are needed.

item.title: A shopper checks her purchase using the avocado retail ripeness checker.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Avocado 'ripeness scanner' trial aims to help customers choose the perfect fruit
item.description: An Australian-first avocado "ripeness scanner" is being trialled, with the aim of telling customers exactly when their avocados will be ready to eat.

item.title: This Easter my heart is with the casualties and refugees in Lebanon
item.description: Lebanese families have been told to flee homes that they might never be able to return to, and families are setting up tents for an uncertain and dangerous life on the streets of Beirut all because of a conflict not of their making, brought about by another state.

item.title: Art created to help Papunya children read in their language goes on display at National Library of Australia
item.description: More than 40 years ago, a program was created to help Papunya children in remote NT learn to read in Pintupi-Luritja languages. Those books are now an insight into the people, stories, history and culture of the Papunya and are on display at the National Library of Australia.

item.title: FluMist nasal spray vaccine is available for use in Australia, but only some children will get it for free
item.description: It's been available in the northern hemisphere for years, now Australian children can receive a nasal spray vaccine for the flu.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of Top Gun, bowling underarm and your weight on the Moon
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Iran's downing of US jet delivers President Donald Trump's quagmire moment
item.description: This is a moment when the US either goes deeper into this war or makes a clear decision to end it.

item.title: Why 18-year-old Daniel Williams is among the rising stars of Australian athletics
item.description: Teenagers Gout Gout and Cameron Myers are already creating an impact on the international stage, helping to inspire the likes of 18-year-old Daniel Williams to follow in their footsteps.

item.title: Interest in novated leases for electric vehicles soaring in Australia amid fuel crisis
item.description: With the petrol bowser continuing to hurt wallets, more Australians than ever are considering purchasing an electric vehicle through a novated lease.

item.title: Outback Queensland's Nindigully Pub changes hands after 25 years
item.description: After turning "the worst pub you ever walked into" into a tourism award winner, Stephen Burns is stepping away from managing the Nindigully Pub.

item.title: Demons star Kysaiah Pickett praised after brilliant start to AFL season
item.description: Ed Langdon says Melbourne teammate Kysaiah Pickett is flourishing in his midfield role, as the Demons continue life under new coach Steven King.
